q: When two or more electromotive sources are connected in series, the resultant emf is the ___ sum of the individual emfs.
In the ___ arrangement, the currents leaving the batteries are in the same direction.
For batteries in series aiding, the resultant emf is $E_1 + ___$.
The total internal resistance of batteries in series aiding is $___$.
Two batteries in series aiding can be regarded as a single battery having an emf of $___$ and internal resistance $(r_1 + r_2)$.
In the ___ arrangement, the emfs of the batteries are in opposition.
The resultant emf in series opposing is the ___ between the individual emfs.
In series opposing, if $E_1$ is greater than $E_2$, the resultant emf is $___$.
The total internal resistance of two batteries in series opposing is still $___$.
When two batteries having equal emfs and equal internal resistances are connected in parallel, the resultant emf is the emf $E$ of a ___ battery.
In parallel combination, the ___ terminals of the batteries are connected together.
The resultant internal resistance of two identical batteries in parallel is $___$.
The current flowing through each battery in a parallel combination of two identical batteries is $___$.
Otherwise than connecting same polarities in parallel, batteries may be ___.
